title: Debugging session 2027-06-22
body: Started investigating the division by zero failure in the calculator library. Test test_division_by_zero consistently fails in tests/test_calc.py. The current divide function in src/calculator.py does not handle divisor == 0. I tried adding a simple check: if divisor == 0: return None. However, the test still fails because the assertion expects a ZeroDivisionError to be raised. Returning None does not satisfy that expectation. Need to either raise the proper exception or adjust the test case. This is an incomplete fix.

title: Performance review of multiplication
body: Noticed that the multiply function used a naive loop over addition. Refactored to use the * operator, resulting in a 30% speed improvement. All existing tests pass.
